project.title: XIAP promotes metastasis of bladder cancer cells by ubiquitylating YTHDC1
project.projectDescription: X-linked inhibitor of apoptosis protein (XIAP), a member of the IAP family, is overexpressed in a variety of tumors and plays an important role in tumor progression. Increasing evidence suggests that XIAP promotes metastasis of bladder cancer but the underlying mechanism is not very clear. The RNA N6-methyladenosine (m6A) reader YTHDC1 regulates RNA splicing, nuclear transport, and mRNA stability and is a potential tumor target; however, its ubiquitin E3 ligase has not been described. In this study, screening of proteins that specifically interact with XIAP identified YTHDC1 to be its degradation substrate. Ectopic overexpression of XIAP promoted degradation of YTHDC1, and knockout of XIAP upregulated YTHDC1, which inhibited metastasis of bladder cancer. Furthermore, YTHDC1 reduced expression of matrix metalloproteinase-2 (MMP-2) by destabilizing its mRNA. These experiments revealed that XIAP promotes ubiquitination of YTHDC1, positively regulating expression of MMP-2 and promoting metastasis of bladder cancer. Collectively, these findings demonstrate that XIAP is a critical regulator of YTHDC1 and pinpoint the XIAP/YTHDC1/MMP-2 axis as a promising target for the treatment of bladder cancer.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: The target plasmids were transiently transfected into cells at a ratio of 1:1. After 24 h of transfection, the proteins were co-expressed in UMUC3 cells. The protein concentration in the whole cell lysate was detected by BCA and the samples were divided into Input and IP. The IP samples were co-incubated with the tag magnetic beads (anti-HA-tag: M180-11; Anti-DDDDK-tag: M185-11R, MBL) for 1 h at 4℃, and then the beads were washed 5 times with cell lysate. Protein complexes is resuspended with boiling buffer and boiled at 100°C for 5 min
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The results of the search analysis of the mass spectrometry data give the signal intensity value (Intensity) of each peptide in different samples. In order to eliminate systematic errors in the amount of sample loaded in mass spectrometry for different samples, the relative quantification of peptides needs to be corrected using a median normalization method (NR). Calculate the relative quantification value of the protein. Protein relative quantification is expressed as the median of the relative quantification of the specific peptide corresponding to the protein
